title: Sloanea obtusa
taxonomy: Sloanea obtusa is classified as a species of plant in the family Erysaceae. However, this classification may not be entirely accurate as some sources place it within the family Sapindaceae. The genus Sloanea is part of the family Erysaceae in some classification systems, while in others, it belongs to the family Sapindaceae. Further research is needed to determine the most accurate classification for this species. It is also known as the 'Pride of India' or 'Bauhinia obtusa'.
uses: Sloanea obtusa is native to tropical regions and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and bark have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In traditional medicine, Sloanea obtusa is often used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of its medicinal uses and potential health benefits.

intro: Sloanea obtusa is a species of plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae, commonly known as the 'Sloanea obtusa' or 'Malayan coral tree'. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. Bioactive constituents of Sloanea obtusa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Sloanea obtusa is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make tinctures and capsules, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
